spec_match.rationale: Chromium-catches-up feature — the spec already documents the behaviour, so the value of the demo is the interop story. The concept exercises one corner but the before/after comparison isn't always crisp.

interactive.rationale: Has 2 interactive control(s) wired to JS but the surface is thin — a single button or readout is barely past the static-card threshold.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Either the feature-index blurb or the concept lede is missing — can't verify alignment.

edge_cases.rationale: Some fallback / error handling is present but corner cases like removed-element behaviour, denied permission flow, or no-support browsers aren't all covered.

references.rationale: No chromestatus.com/feature/<id> link on the concept page — CLAUDE.md rule #3 says every page must carry the self-heal reference, not rely on the parent feature index.

design_system.rationale: Uses a static-card class pattern that CLAUDE.md flags as anti-pattern — refactor to a stage + controls layout.

openQuestions.title: Cover the spec aspect: shadow DOM crossing the boundary
openQuestions.detail: The non-tree-scoped-container-name-matching feature's headline framing — @container name matching ignores tree scope, so a container in a shadow tree can be matched by names declared in the outer document — calls out "shadow DOM crossing the boundary". This concept page does not exercise that, so the spec story is incomplete from this page alone. Either extend this demo or land a sibling concept dedicated to it.

openQuestions.title: Cover the spec aspect: nested component design-system scenarios
openQuestions.detail: The non-tree-scoped-container-name-matching feature's headline framing — @container name matching ignores tree scope, so a container in a shadow tree can be matched by names declared in the outer document — calls out "nested component design-system scenarios". This concept page does not exercise that, so the spec story is incomplete from this page alone. Either extend this demo or land a sibling concept dedicated to it.

openQuestions.title: Add real controls so the demo isn't a styled code card
openQuestions.detail: Concept pages are supposed to be hands-on. With only 0 button(s), 1 input(s), 0 select(s), 1 range slider(s), the surface is thin. Add at least one slider, picker, or interactive readout so visitors can actually probe the API from the page.

openQuestions.title: Add the chromestatus.com/feature link to this page
openQuestions.detail: Per CLAUDE.md rule #3, every concept page must include the https://chromestatus.com/feature/5194034339512320 link directly — it is the self-heal anchor used by the slug-recovery script. Right now the page links only back to the parent feature index.

openQuestions.title: Refactor away from the static-card pattern
openQuestions.detail: Page uses the static-card layout that CLAUDE.md flags as the anti-pattern from the v130-v144 backfill regression. Restructure into a stage + controls + readout block.

openQuestions.title: Cover at least one realistic failure mode
openQuestions.detail: Add feature detection at the top of the script and surface the result. Handle the permission-denied / API-rejection case in the catch branch with a visible message. If the feature is flag-gated or origin-trial, show explicitly what the page does when the API is absent.

openQuestions.title: Align the concept lede with the feature-index blurb
openQuestions.detail: The blurb on the feature index sells one promise; the concept lede frames a different angle. Either reword the lede to echo the blurb's keywords (so visitors recognise they're in the right place) or update the parent blurb.

summary: design-system under non-tree-scoped-container-name-matching: 0 pass / 5 partial / 1 fail. Interactive surface is thin, design-system drift, spec story is partial — see open questions for the angles still missing.
